name: Reduce to General Relativity in tested regimes
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the predictions of General Relativity wherever GR has been experimentally tested: solar-system gravimetry, lunar laser ranging, binary pulsars, and the inspiral phases of compact-object mergers. Numerically: parameterized post-Newtonian (PPN) parameter gamma must equal 1 to better than ~2 x 10^-5 (Cassini Shapiro-delay), beta must equal 1 to ~10^-4, and the inspiral waveforms of GW150914 and subsequent binary black-hole mergers must be reproduced within LIGO/Virgo measurement precision.
limit: Curvature scales from solar-system to compact-binary inspiral; weak-to-moderate field. Strong-field interior of black holes and big-bang singularity are NOT in this benchmark - they are precisely where GR is expected to break down.

regime.description: Length scales from ~10^4 m (lab gravity tests) to ~10^11 m (solar system) to ~10^9-10^7 m (binary inspiral). Curvature radii large compared to Planck length.

what_it_excludes: Any modified-gravity proposal that predicts deviations from GR larger than the Cassini PPN bound, or that distorts the inspiral chirp of binary mergers beyond LIGO sensitivity. This kills most large-distance modifications of gravity that try to explain dark matter or dark energy without paying for the modification with a screening mechanism that hides on solar-system scales.

parameterization.name: Parameterized Post-Newtonian (PPN) framework, plus parameterized post-Keplerian (PPK) for binary pulsars and TIGER-style parameterized GW deviations for compact-binary inspiral.
parameterization.reference: Will, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014); Damour and Deruelle, Ann. Inst. H. Poincare A 44, 263 (1986); LVC, PRD 84, 062003 (2011) (TIGER).
parameterization.summary: PPN expresses the most general weak-field, slow-motion metric around a spherical mass in terms of ten dimensionless parameters: gamma (space curvature per unit mass), beta (nonlinear superposition of gravity), xi (preferred-location), alpha_1, alpha_2, alpha_3 (preferred-frame), zeta_1, zeta_2, zeta_3, zeta_4 (conservation-law violations). GR predicts gamma = beta = 1 and all others = 0.

procedural.predicted_observable: Each PPN parameter maps to a class of weak-field tests. gamma controls Shapiro delay and light deflection; beta controls perihelion precession (combined with gamma); alpha_1, alpha_2, alpha_3 control preferred-frame effects in the solar system and pulsars; ppk Q dot and other PPK timing parameters constrain dipolar GW emission in binaries.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: gamma - 1 = (2.1 +/- 2.3) x 10^-5 (Cassini Shapiro delay, Bertotti et al. 2003); |beta - 1| < 8 x 10^-5 (Mercury perihelion + LLR); |alpha_1| < 10^-4, |alpha_2| < 10^-7, |alpha_3| < 4 x 10^-20 (binary pulsar timing, solar-spin alignment, pulsar self-acceleration). For GW: bounds on graviton mass m_g < 1.27 x 10^-23 eV/c^2 (LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3) and on dispersion / propagation deviations.
framework_inputs: Static, isotropic metric to first post-Newtonian order around a spherical mass: framework must produce g_00 and g_ij in standard PPN gauge.
Post-Keplerian timing of a relativistic binary: framework must produce Damour-Deruelle parameters (k, gamma_PPK, P_b dot, r, s).

Compact-binary GW inspiral waveform: framework must produce h(t) at 3.5PN or higher accuracy in the inspiral band, parameterized so that LIGO/Virgo's pyRing/TIGER pipelines can extract deviation coefficients delta phi_i.
procedural.notes: PPN extraction is a closed-form computation once the metric is in hand. PPK is a fitting problem against a published timing solution. GW waveform comparison is research-grade code (LALSuite, pyRing) but the procedure is well-defined.

primary_references.citation: Will, 'The Confrontation between General Relativity and Experiment', Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (2014)
Bertotti, Iess, Tortora, 'A test of general relativity using radio links with the Cassini spacecraft', Nature 425, 374 (2003)
Abbott et al. (LIGO/Virgo), 'Tests of General Relativity with GWTC-3', PRD 103, 122002 (2021)
